evidence.title: fetched page
evidence.content: Integrate Slack – Notion Help Center Play Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing. Please watch it on YouTube Notion's integration with Slack enables seamless communication between the two platforms. You can send Slack messages to Notion databases and receive Slack notifications for Notion activities. Read on to learn about the different features of the integration and how you can set them up. Set up Slack integration To receive Slack notifications about things that happen in Notion: Go to Settings in your sidebar → Notifications . Find Slack notifications . Click Add new account and go through the authentication flow. To set up Slack notifications as part of a database automation: Select ••• at the top of a database. Select Automations and Add trigger . Then choose Page added or Property edited → Send Slack notification to... Select a Slack channel. If you haven't already, you'll be asked to asked to connect your Slack account. Notion AI can find answers to your questions based on information from your Slack conversations. Learn more about this feature here → Send a Slack message to a Notion database When Slack messages turn into an action item or a new idea, send them right to a Notion database so they don't get lost. Here's how: Enter the command /notion create into Slack, or click ⋮ next to any Slack message → Send to Notion . You may need to select More message shortcuts... to find this option. Search for the database where you'd like this new page to live or paste the link to the database. Then give the page a title. Select Add property to add more information to your new page based on the database’s existing properties. Check the box next to Confirm page creation publicly if you’d like others in your Slack discussion to be notified of the new database item you’ve created. Click Save . Note: The /notion create command only works at the Slack channel level, and not inside of Slack threads. File a Notion task in Slack To create a Notion task directly from Slack: Enter the command /notion task into Slack, or click ⋮ next to any Slack message → Create task in Notion . You may need to select More message shortcuts... to find this option. Search for the project where you'd like this new task to live or paste the link to the Notion project. You can give the task a name, an assignee, status, due date, and description. Check the box next to Confirm page creation publicly if you’d like others in your Slack discussion to be not

evidence.title: Slack - Miro Help Center Miro & Slack Integration | Slack Marketplace Slack Integration | miroslavpejic85/mirotalk | DeepWiki How to Integrate Slack with Miro - livedemo.ai How to connect Miro and Slack - App Integrations - Stackreaction Miro Slack Integration (2026): Setup Guide & Use Cases ...
evidence.content: Slack App integration is set up by a user for their own profile. To enable the integration , open your Miro Profile settings. Getting to Profile settings from Miro dashboard Switch to the Integrations tab, find Miro Feed ( Slack App) and click Connect: Connecting Slack App Another option is to enable it right in the Notifications tab: Enabling Slack ... See full list on help. miro .com Customize the feed that you receive by choosing the events you'd like to be notified about. You can keep track of the following events: 1. invitees sign up 2. someone requests access to a team or a board 3. you are invited to a project 4. a board is shared with you 5. there's a new comment on your board or a reply to your comment on a board 6. some... See full list on help. miro .com When someone requests access to your board you can grant in right in Slack . Choose the option and click the button: Granting access to a board in the Slack channel See full list on help. miro .com The latest version of Miro Slack app unfurls links to Miro boards by adding the board names, descriptions, and board thumbnails. Board name, description, and thumbnail in Slack channel Reinstall your Slack integration to get access to the feature: go to Miro Profile settings > Integrations and click Log out next to Miro Feed ( Slack App). Then click... See full list on help. miro .com When posting a board link in Slack you will see a notification showing the users that don't have access to the board. You can easily invite them to the board right via Slack . Feel free to make the board publicso that anyone with the link can view/comment it. Sharing a Miro board from within Slack See full list on help. miro .com You can use the Miro shortcut to create a board from within Slack . Search Miro and choose Create a board. Creating a board from Slack Enter a title for the board, select a Miro team, and add a short message to send along with the link to the newly created board within Slack . Setting parameters for a new board in Slack Once the board is created, the... See full list on help. miro .com To disable the integration , go to theProfile settings > Integrations and click Log out: Disabling Miro Feed To remove the app from Slack completely, open Miro channel settings in Slack , click Configuration. Configuration of the Miro App for Slack You'll be redirected to the Miro app Settings page. Scroll down, find your name in the list of authoriz... See full list on help. miro .com 1. If a user adds Miro to Slack , will Miro be able to read their Slack channels? - No, Miro will just view basic information about public channels in the workspace. It means that Miro will be able to read the list of channel names and won't be able to read the channel messages. 2. I am getting 'Something went wrong' message when trying to connect t... See full list on help. miro .com Miro and Slack work to simplify your work and maximize productivity, so you can focus on the things that matter. With the Miro app for Slack , you can track all of your Miro notifications and activity anytime without interrupting your workflow. Apr 20, 2026 · Purpose This document describes the Slack integration feature in MiroTalk P2P, which enables users to create meeting rooms directly from Slack using slash commands. The integration provides a secure webhook endpoint that verifies Slack requests and generates instant meeting room URLs. Sep 5, 2025 · Guide Click the '+Add apps' button. Search for ' Miro ' and click 'Add'. Click 'Add to Slack '. Click 'Allow' to connect your Miro account to Slack . That's it! You have granted the necessary permissions for Miro to access your Slack workspace. Integrating Slack with Miro enhances your collaborative efforts by allowing you to manage and share Miro boards directly within Slack . How to connect Miro and Slack Miro and Slack integration is available through workflow automation tools – Make.com, Zapier, Pabbly Connect or 3 more. With the combination of 12 Miro triggers and 197 Slack actions, you can create thousands of integration workflows to automate your work. Miro and Slack are both used by product teams — integrating them removes duplicated status updates and keeps work visible across both tools. Miro lists it as a native integration , so setup takes minutes rather than Zapier workarounds.

cases.input: Compare Notion and ClickUp on collaboration capabilities and calculate the estimated annual cost for 20 users using each product's lowest publicly listed paid team plan, billed annually in USD.
cases.answer:
Capability comparison:

- Notion / collaboration capabilities: unverified
- ClickUp / collaboration capabilities: unverified
- Notion / annual cost for 20 users: verified_present [E6]
- ClickUp / annual cost for 20 users: verified_present [E7]

Cost:
- Notion (Plus): $10.0/user/mo x 20 x 12 = $2400/yr [E6]
- Notion (Business): $20.0/user/mo x 20 x 12 = $4800/yr [E7]

Recommendation:
The verified evidence does not establish a unique winner. Confirm the unverified items that matter to your team before deciding.

evidence.title: fetched page
evidence.content: Notion vs ClickUp — I Spent $200 Testing These (2026) Notion vs ClickUp — I Spent $200 Testing These (2026) by Fahim Joharder | Last updated Apr 1, 2026 Winner 4.5 20,000+ Free Templates Built-in AI Writing Assistant All-in-One Workspace Drag & Drop Page Builder Unlimited Page History (Paid) Generous Free Plan Paid Plans from $10/user/month TRY NOW Runner Up 3.5 Built-in Time Tracking 100+ Automation Templates Gantt Charts & Timelines ClickUp Brain AI Assistant 1,000+ Integrations Free Forever Plan Paid Plans from $7/user/month TRY NOW 📊 Our Test Results: 🎯 Note Taking Speed: Notion 45 seconds vs ClickUp 70 seconds to create a structured note — Notion wins ⚡ Project Setup Time: Notion 3 minutes vs ClickUp 5 minutes for a new project board — Notion wins 🔒 Task Management Depth: Notion 6 task views vs ClickUp 15+ task views — ClickUp wins 📝 Time Tracking: Notion requires third-party apps vs ClickUp has built-in tracking — ClickUp wins 🤖 AI Features: Notion AI writes and summarizes vs ClickUp Brain manages tasks — Notion wins Choosing between Notion vs ClickUp can feel overwhelming. Both are powerful productivity tools that promise to help you stay organized. But they take very different approaches to task management, note taking, and project management. Notion is an all in one workspace built for personal notes, databases, and team collaboration. ClickUp is project management software designed to manage projects, track time, and handle complex tasks. I tested both platforms for four weeks to give you a full comparison. By the end, you will know exactly which tool fits your needs. Overview To give you the most accurate comparison, I tested Notion and ClickUp side by side. I used each platform for project management, note taking, and team communication. I created databases, set up kanban boards, and assigned tasks to team members. I also tested each tool’s free plan and paid plan to see which offers more value. This Notion vs ClickUp review shares my firsthand experience to help you make the right choice. Try Notion for free What is Notion? Notion is a note taking app that doubles as a project management platform. It gives you a single platform to create pages, build databases, and organize ideas. You can use Notion for personal notes, to do lists, journal entries, and meeting notes . Notion users also build wikis, contents tables, and collaborative workspace tools for their teams.
